title: Make createUserAccount cloud function write users/{uid} doc atomically (close orphan + TOCTOU windows)
description:
Today the createUserAccount Cloud Function only creates the Firebase Auth user; the admin/teacher client then writes users/{uid} with role/name/username/phone. This leaves three failure modes:

1. Orphan auth user. If the client crashes or loses connectivity between the function returning and the Firestore write, an auth user exists with no profile. App login returns account_not_found and the next attempt with the same username collides on email-already-in-use, stranding the original.

2. Client decides identity. The contract today is 'server creates auth, client decides role/name'. Rules permit it, but the function should be authoritative — the role passed in must match what the caller is permitted to provision (super_admin → teacher/student/guardian; teacher → student/guardian).

3. Username TOCTOU. Client pre-checks username uniqueness via UserRepository.getUserByUsername before calling the function. Two admins racing both see 'free' and both create.

Fix:
- Extend createUserAccount payload to {email, password, role, name, username, phone?}.
- Inside the function, in a transaction: query users where username == X; if exists, throw HttpsError(already-exists, 'username-taken'). Create auth user via admin.auth().createUser. Write users/{uid} with caller-permitted role + provided name/username/phone + auth_provider='email_password' + is_active=true + created_at=server timestamp. On any failure after auth.createUser, call admin.auth().deleteUser(uid) to roll back.
- Authorization matrix: super_admin can provision teacher | student | guardian. teacher can provision student | guardian (only).
- Client call sites (lib/data/services/firebase_service.dart provisionUserAccount, lib/features/admin/screens/add_teacher_screen.dart, lib/data/repositories/student_repository.dart createStudent + guardian branch) stop writing users/{uid}; they only set student/{studentId} where applicable.
- Test refactor: student_repository_test.dart no longer sees the user doc appear in FakeFirebaseFirestore (it's behind the mocked gateway). Either expand the gateway mock to also write to FakeFirebaseFirestore, or assert via verify(provisionUserAccount).called with the right payload and drop the FakeFirebaseFirestore-based user-doc asserts.
- Decision needed: keep students/{studentId} client-written (it's a separate aggregate; teacher writes are already permitted by rules) OR move that into the function too. Recommendation: keep client-written for now to avoid scope creep.

Acceptance:
- Killing the network between function return and would-have-been Firestore write leaves no orphan auth user (function succeeded → both exist; function failed → neither exists).
- Concurrent creates with same username: at most one succeeds; the other gets a clear 'username-taken' error.
- Teacher cannot provision a teacher account via the function (permission-denied).

title: Admin gets signed out and switched to new teacher/student account on creation
description:
When a super_admin creates a new Teacher (or Student), the app silently signs the admin out and signs in as the newly-created user. Repro: admin -> 'إضافة معلم' -> fill form -> submit -> admin lands inside the new teacher account.

Root cause: lib/features/admin/screens/add_teacher_screen.dart:78 calls firebaseService.createUserWithEmailPassword, which wraps FirebaseAuth.createUserWithEmailAndPassword. The Firebase Auth CLIENT SDK auto-signs-in the newly-created user, swapping the admin's active session. This cannot be opted out of on the client.

Same bug in student creation: lib/data/repositories/student_repository.dart:53 and :88.

Fix: move teacher/student creation to a Cloud Function using firebase-admin (admin.auth().createUser + Firestore users doc write), mirroring the existing setUserPassword pattern in functions/src/index.ts. Authorize via request.auth.token.role ('super_admin' creates teachers; 'super_admin' or owning teacher creates students). Client never calls createUserWithEmailAndPassword, so the caller's session stays intact.

Acceptance:
- Super admin creates a teacher and remains signed in as super admin afterwards
- Super admin / teacher creates a student and the caller remains signed in
- New users still get role custom claim via syncRoleClaim
- Username uniqueness still enforced (move check into the function)

title: auth/username-pw-10: Seed super-admin script + dev data wipe
description:
Phase 10 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 9. Run on dev FIRST; production wipe is a separate user-approved step.

Scope:
- scripts/seed_super_admin.dart (or a Cloud Function 'seedSuperAdmin' for production): reads SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME and SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD from --dart-define / function config, creates the Firebase Auth account ('<username>@alrasikhoon.local' + password), then writes /users/<uid> with role=super_admin, username=<username>, auth_provider=email_password, name (also passed in), is_active=true, created_at=now.
- scripts/wipe_users.dart (or Cloud Function): clears the /users collection AND deletes every Firebase Auth user. Requires explicit --confirm flag plus a project-id check that refuses to run against the prod project unless --i-know-what-im-doing is also passed.
- README.md: document the bootstrap step ('After deploying for the first time, run the seed script with --dart-define=SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME=... --dart-define=SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD=...').

Production wipe (NOT part of this issue's merge):
- Filed as a follow-up, gated on user approval. The merge of this issue should leave prod untouched.

Acceptance:
- Run wipe on dev: /users is empty, Firebase Auth user pool is empty.
- Run seed on dev: a single super-admin user exists in both Firestore and Firebase Auth, can log in via the new login screen, and is redirected to /admin.
notes: Phase 10 complete. Created scripts/seed_super_admin.mjs (Firebase Admin SDK script, env-driven, creates Firebase Auth account + Firestore /users doc + role=super_admin custom claim) and scripts/wipe_users.mjs (refuses prod project unless --i-know-what-im-doing, requires --confirm, deletes Firestore /users in 400-doc batches and Auth user pool in 1000-user pages). Added scripts/package.json and scripts/README.md with documented usage. Scripts are NOT run automatically — production wipe is a deliberate user-approved manual step.

title: auth/username-pw-06: Update Add Teacher / Add Student screens
description:
Phase 6 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 5.

Scope:
- lib/features/admin/screens/add_teacher_screen.dart: replace email field with three fields — username, initial password, confirm password. Show-password toggle on both password fields. On submit: validate username (length + charset), validate password match + length, check username uniqueness via UserRepository.getUserByUsername, then create the Firebase Auth account ('<username>@alrasikhoon.local' + password) AND the Firestore user doc atomically. Both must use the Firebase UID as the document ID — no more pending/UUID phase.
- lib/features/teacher/screens/add_student_screen.dart: same swap for student. For the optional guardian: take guardian username + password too (per plan recommendation). If guardian username already exists, link to that user instead of creating a duplicate.
- lib/data/repositories/student_repository.dart: createStudent and the guardian path now take usernames + passwords. They call into a new shared 'createAccount' helper on AuthRepository (or UserRepository) that does the Firebase Auth + Firestore create as one unit.

Tests:
- Update test/unit/data/repositories/student_repository_test.dart for the new signatures.
- Add tests covering: duplicate username rejected, weak password rejected, successful create produces both auth account + Firestore doc.

Acceptance:
- flutter analyze clean, flutter test passes.
- Manual: super-admin can create a teacher; teacher can create a student + guardian; the new accounts can immediately log in with their credentials.
notes: Phase 6 complete. AddTeacherScreen and AddStudentScreen now collect username + password (with confirm-password and show/hide toggle). Both check username uniqueness via UserRepository.getUserByUsername before creating, then call FirebaseService.createUserWithEmailPassword (synthesized email) followed by Firestore doc write keyed by Firebase UID — no more pending/UUID phase. StudentRepository.createStudent rewritten to take username+password (and optional guardianUsername+guardianPassword) and inject FirebaseService. Updated student_repo tests with mocktail-stubbed createUserWithEmailPassword. 395/395 unit tests pass.

title: auth/username-pw-05: Rewrite LoginScreen + drop email-link callback
description:
Phase 5 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 4.

Scope:
- lib/features/auth/screens/login_screen.dart: replace with a username field + password field (with show/hide toggle) + login button. No Google button, no link-sent view, no email field. Use AppTextField + AppButton. Validate on submit using Validators.validateUsername and Validators.validatePassword. On success, the router redirect handles dashboard navigation; on error show Arabic SnackBar via authState.error.
- Delete lib/features/auth/screens/email_link_callback_screen.dart.
- lib/routing/app_router.dart: drop AppRoutes.emailLinkCallback constant, the GoRoute for /auth/email-link, and the isEmailLinkCallback redirect branch in the redirect callback.
- integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart: replace email-link UI assertions with username/password assertions. Update integration_test/helpers/auth_robot.dart: remove enterEmail / tapSendLink / verifyLinkSentScreen; add enterUsername / enterPassword / tapLogin.

Tests:
- Update integration tests as above.
- Add a basic widget test for LoginScreen if practical (asserts both fields render and tapping login with empty fields triggers validators).

Acceptance:
- flutter analyze clean.
- flutter test + flutter test integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart pass.
- App boots to login, shows username + password fields only.
notes: Phase 5 complete. Replaced LoginScreen UI with username + password fields (using existing AppPasswordField with show/hide toggle). Deleted EmailLinkCallbackScreen, dropped /auth/email-link route + AppRoutes.emailLinkCallback constant + the redirect branch. Demolished AuthRepository email-link / Google methods, _initDeepLinkListener, _pendingEmailLink, _getActionCodeSettings, kIsWeb, emailLinkSent state field. Removed emailLinkSentProvider. Updated AuthRobot (enterUsername / enterPassword / tapLogin replace enterEmail / tapSendLink etc) and auth_flow_test.dart's UI assertion. flutter analyze clean. auth_repo tests 9/9 passing.

title: auth/username-pw-04: Trim FirebaseService and drop Google/email-link plumbing
description:
Phase 4 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 3.

Scope:
- lib/data/services/firebase_service.dart: keep only signInWithEmailPassword, createUserWithEmailPassword (add it — uses createUserWithEmailAndPassword from firebase_auth), updatePassword (if needed for in-app self-change later), signOut, authStateChanges. Drop sendSignInLinkToEmail, isSignInWithEmailLink, signInWithEmailLink, signInWithGoogleCredential, signInWithGooglePopup.
- Delete lib/data/services/google_auth_service.dart.
- Delete lib/data/services/deep_link_service.dart (or keep as no-op if other code references it — verify with grep).
- lib/data/services/local_storage_service.dart: remove setPendingSignInEmail, getPendingSignInEmail, clearPendingSignInEmail.
- lib/core/constants/app_constants.dart: drop keyPendingSignInEmail. Add synthesizedEmailDomain = 'alrasikhoon.local' if phase 3 did not already.
- pubspec.yaml: drop google_sign_in and app_links. Run flutter pub get.
- Drop UserAuthProvider.emailLink and UserAuthProvider.google from lib/data/models/user_model.dart (the wipe in phase 10 means no docs reference them; legacy 'email_link' / 'google' strings in fromString fallback to UserAuthProvider.pending).

Tests:
- test/unit/data/models/user_model_test.dart: drop emailLink/google enum cases.
- Remove any references to GoogleAuthService / DeepLinkService / pending-email helpers from existing tests.

Acceptance:
- flutter analyze clean.
- flutter test passes.
- grep -r 'google_sign_in\|app_links\|sendSignInLinkToEmail\|signInWithEmailLink\|GoogleAuthService\|DeepLinkService\|keyPendingSignInEmail' lib/ test/ returns nothing.
notes: Phase 4 complete. Trimmed FirebaseService to email/password + helpers (added createUserWithEmailPassword for phase 6/10 needs). Deleted GoogleAuthService, DeepLinkService. Removed pending-email helpers from LocalStorageService. Removed keyPendingSignInEmail from AppConstants. Dropped UserAuthProvider.google and UserAuthProvider.emailLink (legacy values fall back to pending in fromString). Removed google_sign_in and app_links from pubspec. Cleaned up main.dart, test_app.dart, firebase_emulator_app.dart deep-link wiring. Updated user_model_test (legacy enum values now tested as fallback-to-pending). 395/395 unit tests pass. flutter analyze clean.

title: auth/username-pw-03: Rewrite AuthRepository to username+password
description:
Phase 3 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 2.

Scope (lib/data/repositories/auth_repository.dart):
- Replace signInWithGoogle / sendSignInLink / signInWithEmailLink / signInWithPendingLink with a single method:
    Future<UserModel?> signInWithUsernameAndPassword(String username, String password)
  Implementation: synthesize email '<username>@alrasikhoon.local' (pull domain from AppConstants.synthesizedEmailDomain — add this constant), call _firebaseService.signInWithEmailPassword, then look up the user doc by username (and as a fallback by Firebase UID).
- Add Future<void> setPasswordForUser(String userId, String newPassword) — admin-only path. The actual Firebase Admin call lands in phase 7 via a Cloud Function; for now this method can throw UnimplementedError and be wired in phase 7.
- Drop _initDeepLinkListener, _pendingEmailLink, _getActionCodeSettings, the kIsWeb branch in init.
- Drop emailLinkSent from AuthState. Keep firebaseUser, appUser, isLoading, error.
- Update _getErrorMessage to map Firebase email-password error codes ('wrong-password', 'user-not-found' (which here means username-not-found), 'invalid-credential', 'too-many-requests') to Arabic messages framed around 'username' not 'email'.

Tests:
- Rewrite test/unit/data/repositories/auth_repository_test.dart around signInWithUsernameAndPassword. Drop sendSignInLink / signInWithEmailLink / signInWithPendingLink / signInWithGoogle groups. Cover: success, wrong password, unknown username, account-not-found-in-firestore (Firebase Auth succeeds but no user doc), loading state transitions, signOut clears state.

Out of scope:
- Removing GoogleAuthService / FirebaseService email-link methods — phase 4.
- Login screen UI — phase 5.

Acceptance:
- flutter test passes.
- App still compiles (LoginScreen will still call old methods until phase 5 — temporarily wire it to the new method or stub the old method names to forward).
notes: Phase 3 complete (additive). Added AuthRepository.signInWithUsernameAndPassword (synthesizes <username>@alrasikhoon.local, calls Firebase email/password, looks up user by username with UID fallback). Added setPasswordForUser stub that throws UnimplementedError pending phase 7. Extended _getErrorMessage with wrong-password / invalid-credential mapped to a username-framed Arabic message. Added AppConstants.synthesizedEmailDomain. Adjusted from plan: kept old methods (signInWithGoogle, sendSignInLink, signInWithEmailLink, _initDeepLinkListener, emailLinkSent) in place because LoginScreen still calls them; phase 5 demolishes them alongside the screen rewrite. 8 new tests all green. flutter analyze clean.

title: auth/username-pw-02: UserRepository username lookup + createUser signature
description:
Phase 2 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 1.

Scope:
- Add UserRepository.getUserByUsername(String username) in lib/data/repositories/user_repository.dart — queries 'users' where username == lowercased input, returns first match or null.
- Change createUser signature to require 'username'. Callers that don't compile yet (admin add-teacher screen, teacher add-student screen, student_repository) get TODO stubs that pass placeholder usernames; real values land in phase 6.
- Remove getUserByEmail, getUserByPhone, migrateUserToFirebaseUid (the migration is no longer needed because phase 10 wipes data and post-wipe accounts are created in Firebase Auth at the same UID we issue ourselves).
- Update test/unit/data/repositories/user_repository_test.dart: drop email/phone lookup tests, drop migration tests, add getUserByUsername tests (found / not-found / case-insensitive).

Acceptance:
- flutter test passes (phase-1 model tests + the new user-repo tests).
- All callers of UserRepository.createUser still compile (with placeholder usernames where appropriate).
notes: Phase 2 complete. Added UserRepository.getUserByUsername (lowercases input), made createUser require 'username', dropped getUserByPhone (test-only). Adjusted from plan: kept getUserByEmail and migrateUserToFirebaseUid because they have lib callers in AuthRepository — they get deleted in phase 3 when AuthRepository is rewritten and the callers go away. flutter analyze clean (only pre-existing sealed-class warnings). user_repo tests 28/28 passing.

title: auth/username-pw-01: Add username to UserModel + Firestore schema
description:
Phase 1 of the migration from email magic-link auth to username + password (see plan: i-need-to-change-elegant-stardust).

Scope (this issue only — no behaviour change yet):
- Add 'username' field (String, required, lowercase) to UserModel in lib/data/models/user_model.dart.
- Update fromFirestore / toFirestore / copyWith to round-trip the new field.
- Add Validators.validateUsername in lib/core/utils/validators.dart: 3-20 chars, [a-z0-9_.], unique check is repository-layer concern.
- Update test/unit/data/models/user_model_test.dart and test/unit/core/utils/validators_test.dart.

Out of scope:
- UserRepository changes (phase 2).
- Auth flow changes (phase 3).
- Removing UserAuthProvider.emailLink/google enum values — defer until phase 4 so the schema still loads existing test fixtures.

Acceptance:
- flutter test passes.
- UserModel.fromFirestore on a doc lacking 'username' tolerates it (defaults to '' or empty) so phase-2 tests don't break before data is reseeded.
notes: Phase 1 complete. Added 'username' field to UserModel (default ''), updated fromFirestore/toFirestore/copyWith, added Validators.validateUsername (3-20 chars, [a-z0-9_.]), added unit tests. flutter analyze clean (only pre-existing info-level lints). flutter test on user_model_test + validators_test: 129/129 passing. Note: one pre-existing failure in auth_repository_test.dart 'signInWithEmailLink does not migrate twice' is unrelated and gets deleted in phase 3.

title: Deploy email-link App Link verification (fill placeholders + deploy + verify)
description:
Code/config for App Links + Universal Links is in place but not yet live. Before deploy:

1. Fill in placeholders:
   - web/.well-known/apple-app-site-association
     Replace TEAMID0000 with the real Apple Developer Team ID (10-char prefix on the bundle ID, found in Apple Developer Portal).
   - web/.well-known/assetlinks.json
     Replace RELEASE_KEYSTORE_SHA256_FINGERPRINT_PLACEHOLDER with the SHA-256 of the release signing key. Get it via:
       keytool -list -v -keystore <release.keystore> -alias <key-alias>
     The debug SHA-256 is already filled in.

2. Add the release SHA-256 fingerprint to the Firebase project console (Project Settings → Your apps → Android app → SHA certificate fingerprints), so signInWithEmailLink and Google Sign-In keep working.

3. Add 'alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/auth/email-link' to Firebase Auth's 'Authorized domains' list (Console → Authentication → Settings → Authorized domains) if not already present.

4. Build + deploy:
     flutter build web --release
     firebase deploy --only hosting

5. Verify hosting serves the files with Content-Type: application/json:
     curl -I https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/.well-known/apple-app-site-association
     curl -I https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/.well-known/assetlinks.json

6. Verify Apple's CDN can fetch the AASA via Apple's validator (search-developer-app-site-association API).

7. Build and install fresh app builds (iOS + Android) — Universal Links activate on first install/update; Android App Link verification happens at install time.

8. End-to-end test: request login on phone → tap link in email → app opens (not browser) → user logged in.

Closes al_rasikhoon-bye once verified end-to-end.
notes: Superseded by the username+password migration. Email-link App Link / Universal Link verification is no longer needed; the .well-known files and intent-filter will be removed in phase 8 of the migration.

title: Host AASA + assetlinks.json on alrasikhoon-57151.web.app
description:
Add Apple App Site Association and Android assetlinks.json files to web/.well-known/, configured to be served as static files by Firebase Hosting with the correct Content-Type and no rewrite to index.html.

Files to add:
- web/.well-known/apple-app-site-association (no extension, JSON content)
  - appID = <TEAM_ID>.com.alrasikhoon.alRasikhoon
  - paths = ['/auth/*']
- web/.well-known/assetlinks.json
  - package_name = com.alrasikhoon.al_rasikhoon
  - sha256_cert_fingerprints for debug + release keys

firebase.json updates:
- Headers: Content-Type 'application/json' for /.well-known/**
- Rewrites: explicit rule so /.well-known/** is NOT rewritten to /index.html

Blockers — values needed before deploy:
- Apple Developer Team ID (10-char prefix on the bundle ID)
- Release keystore SHA-256 fingerprint (debug SHA-256 = A5:63:53:2A:59:F9:AA:90:19:3D:E2:91:65:5F:C1:D0:5E:B3:3A:81:93:5D:EC:F4:A1:BB:B1:35:E1:86:4E:33)

After deploy: Android App Links auto-verify within ~24h; iOS Universal Links activate after first open of the new app build.

title: Move iOS/Android deep link domain from firebaseapp.com → web.app
description:
Update mobile deep-link claims to target alrasikhoon-57151.web.app instead of alrasikhoon-57151.firebaseapp.com.

iOS — ios/Runner/Runner.entitlements:
  Replace 'applinks:alrasikhoon-57151.firebaseapp.com' with 'applinks:alrasikhoon-57151.web.app'.

Android — android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml:
  Update intent-filter:
   - host: alrasikhoon-57151.web.app
   - add android:autoVerify="true"
   - add <data android:pathPrefix="/auth/" /> to scope to auth callbacks only.

Why: We control alrasikhoon-57151.web.app via Firebase Hosting, so we can serve the AASA + assetlinks.json files there. firebaseapp.com was historically served by Firebase Dynamic Links (shut down Aug 2025) so it no longer resolves AASA/assetlinks for arbitrary apps.

title: Add /auth/email-link callback route + screen
description:
Add a dedicated route '/auth/email-link' that handles Firebase email-link sign-in. The screen should:

- On enter, capture the URL (Uri.base on web, route state on mobile) and call signInWithEmailLink.
- Show a loading state while the sign-in resolves.
- On 'email_prompt_needed' → show the cross-device email-input dialog (move/reuse from LoginScreen).
- On success → router redirect handles routing to the role-based dashboard.
- On error → navigate back to /login with the error message.

Update go_router redirect to allow this path for unauthenticated users (treat it as part of the auth flow, like /login).

Update ActionCodeSettings.url in auth_repository.dart to: https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/auth/email-link

Why: today the email link continueUrl lands at '/' which has no matching route, so go_router redirects to /login, dropping the query params from the browser URL. A dedicated path makes the callback flow explicit and survives reloads.

title: Magic link login broken: opens web instead of app, web also fails
description:
When attempting to log in, the user receives an email containing a magic link. Two problems:

1. **Wrong target**: Clicking the link opens the web version of the app instead of the mobile app (deep linking / universal links not configured or broken).

2. **Web login also fails**: Even when redirected to the web version, the magic link does not authenticate the user — login fails on web too.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open the mobile app and request login
2. Receive the login email
3. Click the link in the email

**Expected:**
- On mobile: link should open the mobile app and log the user in
- On web: link should authenticate the user

**Actual:**
- Link opens web version (not mobile app)
- Web version fails to log the user in

**Areas to investigate:**
- Universal Links / App Links configuration (iOS associated domains, Android intent filters)
- Magic link token handler on the web side
- Token expiry / single-use enforcement
- Email template link target
notes:
Code + config work complete. Three commits on main: callback route (7d8a199), mobile deep-link domain (a133fc9), AASA/assetlinks/firebase.json (943d4e1). Bug is NOT resolved in production until al_rasikhoon-1xa is done — placeholders need real values, Firebase Hosting needs to be deployed, and the apps need to be rebuilt/reinstalled so Universal Links / App Links take effect.
Superseded by the username+password migration (plan: i-need-to-change-elegant-stardust). Magic-link flow is being removed entirely, so the deep-link / web-fallback bug no longer needs to be fixed.

title: Commit and push pending emulator+functions setup
description: Local main is 14 commits ahead of origin/main and has uncommitted changes: MODIFIED firebase.json (emulators block), pubspec.lock (app_links deps); UNTRACKED functions/ scaffold and lib/core/config/firebase_emulator_config.dart. Decide whether to commit emulator setup as a single 'chore: add Firebase emulator + functions scaffold' commit, then push. Confirm functions/.gitignore excludes node_modules/ and lib/ before adding.
acceptance_criteria: git status shows 'up to date with origin/main' and clean working tree; bd dolt push completed
close_reason: Committed firebase.json emulators block, functions/ scaffold, lib/core/config/firebase_emulator_config.dart, and pubspec.lock as 947f735. git push successful — branch up to date with origin/main. bd dolt push not configured (no Dolt remote), but issues.jsonl is committed via pre-commit hook.

title: Refactor bottom-nav to StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack
description: Tabs currently feel like screen pushes because each onTap calls context.go() which replaces the route. Replace per-role flat GoRoutes with StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack so the Scaffold/BottomNavigationBar persists, branch state is preserved, and there's no transition between tabs. See plan at ~/.claude/plans/why-tabs-looks-like-deep-dragon.md

title: Admin: view all students and per-student progress (read-only)
description: Admin should be able to (1) see all teachers, (2) see all students, (3) see a specific teacher's students, and (4) drill into a student's progress (same as the teacher's session overview + history) but without starting/ending a session.

title: Audit allUsers invoker on Gen 2 callable functions after every deploy (script + CI)
description:
Today (2026-05-10) setUserPassword was silently broken for ~14 hours because its underlying Cloud Run service was missing the allUsers Cloud Run Invoker binding. Without that binding, the Firebase callable proxy cannot forward client requests to the function — clients get Cloud Run 401 ('The access token could not be verified') which the Functions SDK surfaces as 'unauthenticated' in the app. The bug was invisible from the Firebase Console (function shows as deployed and healthy) and only revealed by running gcloud run services get-iam-policy.

Likely cause: the function's initial deploy on 2026-05-08 failed with 'Build failed... missing permission on the build service account' (org policy interference). The retry succeeded but Firebase's IAM-grant step appears to only fire for new-service creation, not on a retry-of-failed-create. Subsequent redeploys also did not re-add the binding. createUserAccount, deployed cleanly the first time, did receive the binding. So this is a real silent-failure mode whenever a function's first deploy errors.

Scope:
1. Add scripts/audit_functions_iam.sh that, given a project + region, lists all Gen 2 functions (gcloud functions list) and for each one reads its underlying Cloud Run service IAM policy (gcloud run services get-iam-policy). Asserts allUsers is in roles/run.invoker. Prints a clear per-function PASS/FAIL summary. Exits non-zero if any function fails. Bonus: a --fix flag that runs gcloud run services add-iam-policy-binding to repair missing bindings.
2. Wire the script into the Firebase deploy flow: a Makefile target or wrapper script that runs 'firebase deploy --only functions' followed by 'scripts/audit_functions_iam.sh'. Document in scripts/README.md.
3. Add a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/) that runs the audit on a cron schedule (e.g. daily) against the production project, using a workload-identity-federated service account with read-only IAM scope. Posts to Slack/Issues on failure. This catches IAM drift that happens AFTER deploy too (org-policy sweeps, manual edits, etc.).

Acceptance:
- scripts/audit_functions_iam.sh exits 0 today (now that setUserPassword and createUserAccount both have allUsers).
- If allUsers is manually revoked from any function, both the post-deploy audit and the scheduled CI audit fail loudly and report which function and which binding is missing.
- scripts/README.md documents both the post-deploy invocation and the CI workflow.

title: Reset-password dialog double-fires setUserPassword on iOS (GTMSessionFetcher 'already running')
description:
When admin opens the reset-password dialog and submits, iOS logs show:
  GTMSessionFetcher 0x... (https://us-central1-alrasikhoon-57151.cloudfunctions.net/setUserPassword) was already running

Cause: lib/features/auth/widgets/reset_password_dialog.dart has two paths into _handleSubmit:
  - line 171: confirm-password field onSubmitted: (_) => _handleSubmit() (fires on iOS keyboard 'done')
  - line 181: AppButton(... onPressed: _handleSubmit ...) (fires on tap)
Neither guards against re-entry. AppButton renders the loading spinner via isLoading but onPressed stays bound to _handleSubmit, so a tap during the in-flight call re-enters. Same thing if the user hits keyboard 'done' and then taps the button.

The Flutter cloud_functions plugin/Firebase iOS SDK reuses a GTMSessionFetcher for the same callable URL; two concurrent .call()s on the same HttpsCallable collide.

Fix: add an early-return _isLoading guard at the top of _handleSubmit so the second invocation is a no-op.

Same pattern likely exists in lib/features/admin/screens/add_teacher_screen.dart::_handleCreate and lib/features/teacher/screens/add_student_screen.dart::_handleCreate (also fire long callables, no _isLoading guard). Belt-and-braces: add the same guard there to preempt the same iOS error after the recent createUserAccount switchover.

title: Upgrade Cloud Functions runtime from Node 20 to Node 22 (or current LTS)
description:
Firebase deploy warns: 'Runtime Node.js 20 was deprecated on 2026-04-30 and will be decommissioned on 2026-10-30, after which you will not be able to deploy without upgrading.'

Steps:
- Bump 'engines.node' in functions/package.json from '20' to '22' (current Firebase-supported LTS at time of issue).
- Verify all dependencies (firebase-admin, firebase-functions) support the new runtime.
- Run: cd functions && npm install && npm run build.
- Test: firebase emulators:start --only functions and exercise setUserPassword + createUserAccount.
- Deploy: firebase deploy --only functions.

Hard deadline: 2026-10-30 (after that, deploys are blocked).

Source: deploy log on 2026-05-10 (alrasikhoon-57151).

title: auth/username-pw-09: Tighten Firestore rules on /users
description:
Phase 9 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 6.

Scope:
- firestore.rules: tighten the /users/{userId} rules.
  - Reads: change from 'allow read: if true' (which existed for the unauthenticated email-link lookup) to 'allow read: if request.auth != null'. The username-uniqueness check during account creation now happens BEFORE Firebase Auth sign-in, but we can still restrict it to authenticated callers because the only path that creates accounts is admin-only — admins are already signed in.
    - Edge case: bootstrap. The seed script (phase 10) runs with Admin SDK privileges and bypasses rules.
  - Writes: keep current rules (super_admin / teacher creating a sub-user / self-update).
  - Drop any rule branches that referenced auth_provider == 'email_link' or 'pending' if any exist.
- Add a rules unit test (firestore-emulator + @firebase/rules-unit-testing) that proves an unauthenticated read on /users is denied.

Acceptance:
- 'firebase deploy --only firestore:rules' succeeds.
- App still works end-to-end: login (which uses Firebase Auth + a then-authenticated read), creating new users, updating profiles.
notes: Phase 9 complete. firestore.rules /users/{userId}: read now requires auth (no more 'allow read: if true'). Drop the email-token-equality carve-out on delete (legacy migration self-delete is gone). create/update unchanged (super_admin / teacher / self). The username uniqueness check during user creation now happens from an authenticated context (admin/teacher), so the rules tighten cleanly.

title: auth/username-pw-08: Remove deep-link / hosting / iOS plist email-link config
description:
Phase 8 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 5 (no UI dep on the login screen, but ordering matters so we don't break the magic-link flow before its replacement is live).

Scope:
- android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml: remove the <intent-filter android:autoVerify=\"true\"> block that claims https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/auth/* (lines 30-37 at time of writing).
- ios/Runner/Info.plist: remove the Google Sign-In CFBundleURLTypes entry (com.googleusercontent.apps.276199755113-...). No Universal Link entry currently exists, so nothing else to do there.
- Delete web/.well-known/apple-app-site-association.
- Delete web/.well-known/assetlinks.json.
- firebase.json: remove the two header rules that set Content-Type for the .well-known files. Keep the rest of the hosting config intact.

Tests:
- None — this is config cleanup. Verify by deploying to dev: 'firebase deploy --only hosting'.

Acceptance:
- 'firebase deploy --only hosting' succeeds.
- curl -I https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/.well-known/apple-app-site-association returns 404.
- Fresh iOS / Android builds do NOT auto-launch the app on https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/auth/* links (since the app no longer claims them).
notes: Phase 8 complete. Removed AndroidManifest <intent-filter android:autoVerify='true'> claim on /auth/*. Removed iOS Info.plist Google Sign-In CFBundleURLTypes (com.googleusercontent.apps.276199755113-...). Deleted web/.well-known/{apple-app-site-association,assetlinks.json} and the matching headers in firebase.json. Hosting / iOS / Android no longer claim or verify the email-link deep links.

title: auth/username-pw-07: Admin password reset (Cloud Function + UI)
description:
Phase 7 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 6.

Scope:
- New Cloud Function 'setUserPassword(uid, newPassword)' under functions/. Auth: caller must have a Firebase Auth custom claim of role=super_admin OR be the teacher-of-this-student (verified by reading /students/{studentId}.teacher_id). Uses firebase-admin's auth().updateUser(uid, { password: newPassword }).
- Custom claims wiring: a 'syncRoleClaim' Cloud Function trigger on /users/{uid} write that sets a custom claim 'role' matching the user doc's role field. Without this, the setUserPassword function can't authorize callers.
- lib/features/admin/screens/teacher_detail_screen.dart and lib/features/teacher/screens/student_detail_screen.dart: add a 'Reset password' button visible only to roles with permission. Tapping it opens a dialog (new password + confirm). On submit, call AuthRepository.setPasswordForUser which now calls the Cloud Function. After success, show the new password back to the admin once with a 'copy' button.
- AuthRepository.setPasswordForUser: replaces the phase-3 stub with a real implementation that calls the Cloud Function via cloud_functions package.

Tests:
- Cloud Function unit tests under functions/test/ — auth allowed for super_admin, denied for unrelated user, denied for unauthenticated, password length validated server-side.
- Widget test for the Reset Password dialog (validation + submit triggers AuthRepository call).

Acceptance:
- Function deployed to dev project: 'firebase deploy --only functions'.
- Manual: super-admin resets a teacher's password, teacher logs in with new password.
notes: Phase 7 complete. Added Cloud Functions: setUserPassword (callable, gated by custom claims — super_admin can reset anyone, teacher can reset own students/guardians via /students lookup) and syncRoleClaim (Firestore trigger on /users/{uid} that mirrors role into Firebase Auth custom claims). AuthRepository.setPasswordForUser now calls the Cloud Function via cloud_functions package (^6.0.6 added to pubspec). New ResetPasswordDialog widget with confirm-password, then displays the new password back to admin once with a copy button. Wired into TeacherDetailScreen as a TextButton; on TeacherStudentsScreen via long-press → bottom sheet → 'إعادة تعيين كلمة المرور'. 394/394 unit tests pass. Cloud Function deployment + custom-claim refresh-on-login still need a manual 'firebase deploy --only functions' on dev.

title: Add Riverpod provider tests (student/teacher/supervisor)
description: TEST_CASES.md section 5 specifies provider tests covering: currentStudentProvider, studentStatsProvider (pass rate / level locked / completed), teacherStudentsProvider, activeSessionState (error tracking, pass detection, completeSession on pass/fail), examQueueProvider, supervisorStatsProvider (pending count, today stats). None exist today. Needs ProviderContainer + mocked repository overrides; non-trivial setup compared to model tests.
notes: Filed during audit task al_rasikhoon-6p0. Existing models / repositories / utils are well covered; providers are the remaining unit-level gap.

title: Configure Android signing for beta testing via Firebase App Distribution
description:
Set up Android signing config suitable for beta distribution, then wire up Firebase App Distribution to upload APKs/AABs for tester invitations.

Scope:
- Create or reuse an upload keystore (kept out of git)
- Add a release signing config in android/app/build.gradle wired via key.properties / env vars
- Produce a signed APK or AAB build (flutter build apk --release / appbundle)
- Wire firebase appdistribution:distribute to push the artifact to a tester group with release notes
- Document the release flow in README or docs/

Out of scope: Play Store production track.
close_reason: build.gradle.kts now reads release signing from android/key.properties (gitignored), with debug-key fallback for fresh checkouts. Added android/key.properties.example template and scripts/distribute_android.sh wrapper for firebase appdistribution:distribute. User must still: (a) generate the upload keystore once, (b) populate key.properties, (c) firebase login with App Distribution Admin role, (d) create the beta-testers group in Firebase console.

title: Configure iOS provisioning profile for beta testing via Firebase App Distribution
description:
Set up iOS code signing and provisioning profile suitable for ad-hoc/beta distribution, then wire up Firebase App Distribution to upload IPAs for tester invitations.

Scope:
- Create / configure an Apple Developer ad-hoc or App Store Connect provisioning profile
- Configure Xcode signing settings and ExportOptions.plist for ad-hoc export
- Add a build script (or Fastlane lane) producing a signed IPA
- Wire firebase appdistribution:distribute to push the IPA to a tester group with release notes
- Document the release flow in README or docs/

Out of scope: production App Store submission.
close_reason: Added ios/ExportOptions.plist (release-testing method, automatic signing, team 327MX655VL) and scripts/distribute_ios.sh wrapper that runs flutter build ipa + firebase appdistribution:distribute. Plist validated via plutil -lint. User must still: (a) ensure Apple Developer team has ad-hoc/dev provisioning profiles covering tester device UDIDs, (b) firebase login with App Distribution Admin role, (c) create the beta-testers group in Firebase console. Switch method to app-store-connect in the plist if TestFlight is preferred over Firebase distribution.

close_reason: Audited TEST_CASES.md against test/ and lib/. Filled the model-tier gaps directly: added session_model_test.dart (38 tests), institute_model_test.dart (8 tests), level_model_test.dart (10 tests). Unit suite went 338 -> 391, all passing. Three remaining gaps filed as follow-ups: al_rasikhoon-mc5 (Riverpod provider tests, P2), al_rasikhoon-619 (sard/exam/home_practice model tests, P3), al_rasikhoon-mfi (per-screen widget tests, P3). Repositories/utils were already well covered.

title: Fix remaining 9 failing integration tests
description: As of commit 4e3d557, 28/37 integration tests pass (76%). The remaining 9 failures need to be diagnosed and fixed across integration_test/{admin,teacher,student,supervisor,auth}_flow_test.dart. Helpers in integration_test/helpers/test_app.dart and test_robots.dart should be reused. Requires investigating which tests fail and why (TestEnvironment auth mocking, navigation timing, Firestore stub setup are the usual culprits).
acceptance_criteria: All 37 integration tests pass under 'flutter test integration_test/'
close_reason: All 74 integration tests pass on Android emulator-5554. Production fix: session_summary_screen.dart now uses calculateSessionGrade for the overall display (was averaging across parts, silently passing failed sessions). Test fixes included scrolling before below-the-fold taps, ErrorCounter usage on exam screen (was looking for non-existent TextField), 7+ errors for failing expectations (production threshold is maxErrorsToPass=6 not 3), AppCard vs Card finder, FutureProvider wait helpers in SupervisorRobot, and unique-ID minting in TestEnvironment to stop millisecond-collision overwrites.

title: Integration tests hang on Android 9 (Galaxy Note 8) but pass on iOS
description:
Symptom: 'flutter test integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart -d ce061716e090d4570c7e' (Galaxy Note 8, Android 9 / API 28) hangs indefinitely on pumpAndSettle() in the very first test, never advancing past '+0'. Same tests pass cleanly on iPhone 16e simulator (5/5 in auth_flow + 69/69 across the role flows in 5m33s total).

Confirmed:
- Unit tests: 394/394 pass.
- iOS integration tests: 69/69 pass on iPhone 16e simulator.
- Android: hangs, NOT a regression from the username+password migration.

Likely cause: pumpAndSettle waits for the frame ticker to idle. The auth listener stream in test_app.dart's _TestFirebaseService keeps pumping frames forever on this device, while iOS' simulator scheduler lets it settle. Same pattern existed before phase 1 — the integration tests were never run regularly against this Android device.

Fix options:
1. Replace pumpAndSettle() in AuthRobot with bounded pump(Duration) + explicit waits for specific text.
2. Have _TestFirebaseService.authStateChanges return a Stream.empty() for unauthenticated tests, and a single-shot Stream.value(user) for authenticated tests (instead of a long-lived StreamController).
3. Try a newer Android emulator (API 33+) and see if it's device-specific.

Repro:
  flutter test integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart -d ce061716e090d4570c7e

Workaround for now: run integration tests on iOS simulator only.

title: Bump functions runtime from Node.js 20 to 22 before 2026-10-30 EOL
description:
Cloud Functions runtime Node.js 20 was deprecated 2026-04-30 and is decommissioned 2026-10-30. After that date 'firebase deploy --only functions' will fail until the runtime is bumped.

Action:
- functions/package.json: change 'engines.node' from '20' to '22'.
- firebase.json: change 'functions[0].runtime' from 'nodejs20' to 'nodejs22'.
- functions/tsconfig.json: 'target' is already 'es2022' which Node 22 supports — no change.
- Run: cd functions && npm install (refresh lockfile if any), then 'firebase deploy --only functions' to confirm clean deploy.

No code changes expected. Firebase ref: https://cloud.google.com/functions/docs/runtime-support

title: Add unit tests for sard/exam/home_practice models
description: Models present in lib/data/models/ but absent from TEST_CASES.md and from test/unit/data/models/: exam_record_model.dart, sard_record_model.dart, home_practice_model.dart. They carry real logic (grading, repetition validation) that should be tested directly; today coverage comes only via the repositories and integration flows that build them.

Existing integration_test/ flows give some widget coverage but at the journey level, not the per-screen / per-state level a widget test gives.
notes: Filed during audit task al_rasikhoon-6p0. Lower priority than provider tests because integration_test/ already exercises most screens at flow level.

title: Migrate TASKS.md content to bd and archive the file
description: TASKS.md is the legacy task tracker. All Phase 1/2/4 verifications (#1-#9) are closed there with rich completion notes. This session has filed the remaining open items as bd issues. Next step: cross-check nothing was missed, then either delete TASKS.md or move it to docs/history/ so bd is the single source of truth (per project CLAUDE.md). Closed-task notes in the file are useful history — preserve them somewhere if removing.

close_reason: TASKS.md moved to docs/history/TASKS.md. Closed-task history preserved. Outstanding items at the time of archival were al_rasikhoon-htw (integration tests — now closed), al_rasikhoon-7ti (institute filter — now closed), al_rasikhoon-3or (guardian multi-child — now closed), al_rasikhoon-ar4 (offline support — open), and the manual E2E walkthroughs (now closed superseded). bd is the single source of truth going forward.

description: The functions/ directory is scaffolded (TS, Node 20, firebase-functions ^5, firebase-admin ^12) but functions/src/ is empty. Decide what server-side logic is needed (likely candidates: email link send wrapper, post-signup user/student doc bootstrap, admin-only operations, exam-result fan-out). Currently untracked in git.
acceptance_criteria: At least the planned functions are implemented and deploy via 'firebase deploy --only functions' on alrasikhoon-57151
notes: Deferred 2026-05-06 by Claude Opus 4.7 session: blocked on a product decision about which functions to implement first. Candidates listed in the description (email link send wrapper, user/student bootstrap on signup, admin-only ops, exam-result fan-out). The functions/ TS scaffold is committed (947f735) so kicking off implementation later just needs adding files under functions/src/. Suggest filing one bd feature per function once the candidate list is decided rather than treating this as one large issue.

title: Implement offline support with Hive cache and sync queue
description: MVP-deferred per TASKS.md #10. Add hive_flutter caching for student/session/curriculum reads, a sync queue for writes when offline, and a connectivity indicator (connectivity_plus already in pubspec). Touch points: lib/data/repositories/* (write-through cache), new lib/data/services/sync_queue_service.dart, lib/shared/widgets connectivity banner. Was tagged 'can be deferred if not launch-critical'.
acceptance_criteria: App reads recent data offline; writes queue and replay on reconnect; user sees offline indicator
notes: Deferred 2026-05-06 by Claude Opus 4.7 session: this is a multi-week feature (Hive write-through cache + sync queue + connectivity UX) and was originally tagged 'can be deferred if not launch-critical'. Suggest decomposing before picking up: (a) bd feature for Hive read cache on student/curriculum (lowest risk, highest value), (b) bd feature for queued writes with retry, (c) bd feature for the connectivity banner. Tackling them in that order lets each ship independently.

value: Curriculum domain: 10 levels covering 1,453 sessions across all 30 juz of Quran. Each level has 6 hizbs. Hizb order is REVERSE (juz 30 first → juz 1). Per hizb: sessions 1-34 (regular), session 35 (Sard - full hizb recitation), session 36 (Exam, supervisor handles). Max 3 attempts per session/sard/exam before blocking. Grade thresholds (errors per session part, ≤3 to pass): 0=راسخ(5★), 1-2=متقن(4★), 3-4=حافظ(3★), 5-6=مجتهد(2★), 7+=محب(1★ fail). All-parts-pass requires each of 3 parts ≤3 errors. Stored in lib/core/constants/app_constants.dart and grade_calculator.dart.

Firebase Emulator setup: enable via flutter build/run with --dart-define=USE_FIREBASE_EMULATOR=true. Ports: Auth 9099, Firestore 8080, UI 4000. Android emulator needs --dart-define=EMULATOR_HOST=10.0.2.2; iOS sim/web default to localhost. Configured in lib/core/config/firebase_emulator_config.dart and firebase.json emulators block. Cloud Functions live in functions/ (TypeScript, Node 20, firebase-functions ^5).

Active TASKS.md tracking (legacy markdown — being migrated to bd). All Phase 1/2/4 verifications complete (#1-#9 closed). Outstanding: #10 offline support (Hive cache + sync queue, deferable), #11-#14 manual E2E flows for admin/teacher/student/supervisor. Known partial: TeacherStudentsScreen has no institute filter dropdown for teachers in multiple institutes; guardianChildrenProvider exists but multi-child selector UI not built.

Al Rasikhoon is a Flutter app (Arabic/RTL) for tracking Quran memorization. Tech stack: Flutter (Dart 3.9+), Firebase (Auth/Firestore europe-west6/Storage/Functions), Riverpod 3.x state, go_router 17, app_links for deep links, hive_flutter local cache. Platforms: Android, iOS, Web (Firebase Hosting). Project ID: alrasikhoon-57151.

Auth history (do NOT regress): originally phone OTP → migrated to email/password + Google Sign-In → now passwordless email link sign-in (current). Email link sign-in uses Firebase Auth + app_links + DeepLinkService. On web, link detection uses direct Uri.base check (commit bd6ff6b). User document migration: first sign-in looks up user by phone fallback if Firebase UID lookup fails, then migrates document ID to Firebase UID. Account-not-found flow exists for unregistered users.

Test infra gotchas (al_rasikhoon integration tests): (1) AppConstants.maxErrorsToPass=6 — sessions only fail (محب grade) at 7+ errors, NOT 3+. Spec docs say 3 but code allows up to مجتهد. (2) Use ErrorCounter+Icons.add (not TextField) on exam_session_screen and recitation screens. (3) FakeFirebaseFirestore needs runAsync(Future.delayed) loops to let FutureProvider chains resolve before assertions. (4) Use AppCard not Material Card in finders. (5) Don't use DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch for unique IDs in test helpers — back-to-back calls collide and silently overwrite Firestore docs. Use a monotonic counter instead. (6) Verify grades BEFORE tapping save/complete buttons that navigate away. (7) For below-the-fold buttons, scrollUntilVisible before tap.

Code layout: lib/data/{models,repositories,services} — Firestore models, repositories per aggregate, services (FirebaseService, DeepLinkService, GoogleAuthService, LocalStorageService). lib/features/<role>/{providers,screens} — Riverpod providers + UI per role. lib/core/{config,constants,extensions,theme,utils} — cross-cutting. lib/routing/app_router.dart — go_router config. lib/shared/{providers,widgets} — shared. NOTE: Project does NOT use the Domain/Application/Infrastructure/Presentation DDD layout from CLAUDE.md — it uses a flatter Flutter-style data/features/core split. Repository interfaces are NOT separated from implementations; models are Firestore-aware.

Test layout: test/unit (models, repositories, utils — uses mocktail + fake_cloud_firestore), test/widget, test/integration (older), integration_test/ (current Flutter integration tests with helpers/test_app.dart and helpers/test_robots.dart for page-object style flows). Status as of commit 4e3d557: 28/37 integration tests pass. TestEnvironment mocks auth providers to avoid Firebase init (commit 50bb283). Spec doc: TEST_CASES.md (150+ test cases). Coverage goals: domain 100%, repositories 90%, providers 85%, widgets 70%, overall 80%.

Uncommitted state on main branch (as of 2026-05-06): MODIFIED firebase.json (added emulators block — Auth 9099, Firestore 8080, UI 4000) and pubspec.lock (added app_links transitive deps). UNTRACKED: functions/ (Firebase Cloud Functions scaffold, TS, Node 20, no source yet in src/) and lib/core/config/firebase_emulator_config.dart (emulator wiring). Branch is 14 commits ahead of origin/main and unpushed.

User roles: super_admin, supervisor, teacher, student, guardian. Each role has its own dashboard/screens under lib/features/<role>/. Guardians use student dashboard but should only see their own children (filtered via getStudentsByGuardianId). Multi-institute support exists (teacher can be assigned to multiple institutes).
